description_long: This sculpture features a detailed bas-relief of a Lamassu, a mythological Assyrian creature with the body of a bull or lion, wings of an eagle, and a human head, set against a rectangular stone slab. In front of the relief stands a full-figure statue of a robed human, possibly a priest or king, holding a golden vessel in one hand. The human figure is adorned with a tall headdress and intricate beard, and some green moss or plant growth is visible on the surfaces, suggesting age or outdoor exposure. The Lamassu's wings and body are finely textured, with visible feather and fur patterns, and the slab base is thick and sturdy. The overall coloration is a weathered stone beige, with the vessel providing a metallic gold accent. The sculpture is monumental and appears to be made primarily of stone, with the vessel possibly metal.
description: A monumental stone sculpture featuring a Lamassu bas-relief and a robed human figure holding a golden vessel, with detailed textures and weathered surfaces.
description_view_0: Front view showing the Lamassu's face and body head-on, with the robed figure and golden vessel clearly visible to the side.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the full length of the Lamassu relief, the robed figure in profile, and the golden vessel in the figure's hand.
description_view_2: Rear view of the robed figure and a narrow side of the Lamassu relief, with visible plant growth along the edge.
description_view_3: Back view showing the full Lamassu relief in profile, with detailed wing and body textures, and the robed figure's headdress just visible above the slab.
attribute_subject_matter: Lamassu mythological creature and a robed human figure
attribute_location_context: No clear environmental context is visible; the sculpture appears isolated, possibly intended for outdoor or public display.
